73643.subject: Ethereum's Stake Queue Outpaces Exits 53,000-to-1
73643.body:
**gm, Defiers! — TL;DR**

* **[Ethereum's](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/ethereum-staking-queue-3-4m-eth-demand-br5lku)**[ staking queue reaches 3.39M ETH against just 64 ETH waiting to exit](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/ethereum-staking-queue-3-4m-eth-demand-br5lku)

* **[MicroStrategy](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/microstrategy-bitcoin-bonds-pivot-lf43s7)**[ buys bonds in a tactical pivot from its Bitcoin-only treasury stance](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/microstrategy-bitcoin-bonds-pivot-lf43s7)

* **[Polkadot](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/polkadot-validator-self-stake-minimum-approved-5lqfj3)**[ approves a 10,000 DOT validator self-stake minimum and 24-hour unbonding](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/polkadot-validator-self-stake-minimum-approved-5lqfj3)

* **[NYT](https://thedefiant.io/news/regulation/cftc-officials-suspended-prediction-markets-nyt-lhyttr)**[ investigation exposes CFTC suspensions over prediction-markets scrutiny](https://thedefiant.io/news/regulation/cftc-officials-suspended-prediction-markets-nyt-lhyttr)

* **[Base](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/base-mcp-agent-gateway-launch-2p8bh1)**[ launches an MCP Agent Gateway, opening DeFi execution to AI agents](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/base-mcp-agent-gateway-launch-2p8bh1)

Bitcoin's corporate-treasury thesis weakened today, while Ethereum's staking-yield thesis posted a new structural high in demand.

**[Ethereum's](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/ethereum-staking-queue-3-4m-eth-demand-br5lku)**[ validator entry queue now holds ](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/ethereum-staking-queue-3-4m-eth-demand-br5lku)**[3.39 million ETH](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/ethereum-staking-queue-3-4m-eth-demand-br5lku)**[ waiting to stake](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/ethereum-staking-queue-3-4m-eth-demand-br5lku), against just **64 ETH** in the exit queue. The 53,000-to-1 ratio is striking on its own; the fact that validators are queueing during a 24-hour drawdown in spot price is more so — this is yield demand, not price-chasing momentum.

On the flipside, **[MicroStrategy](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/microstrategy-bitcoin-bonds-pivot-lf43s7)**[, the corporate-treasury archetype, holding ](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/microstrategy-bitcoin-bonds-pivot-lf43s7)**[843,738 BTC](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/microstrategy-bitcoin-bonds-pivot-lf43s7)**[ worth roughly $65 billion, disclosed bond purchases this week](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/microstrategy-bitcoin-bonds-pivot-lf43s7). **Michael Saylor** framed the move as tactical rebalancing rather than a thesis change, but the fact is that one of the loudest 'never sell' Bitcoin treasury operators is now allocating to fixed income.

Not to be outdone, **[Polkadot](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/polkadot-validator-self-stake-minimum-approved-5lqfj3)**[ governance approved a ](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchain

73642.subject: Daily Pre-IPO Briefing: Anthropic, Cursor, & More
73642.body:
A potential record AI round, a new ARR milestone, and a LOI from the government

StartEngine Private (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VWCpmr7nxtPTW3FryFd7Ys9CZW5b9Bvk5PxW_NMQqrVs3lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3p1W7DT9CD7zX9CyW17zQLh99k_jkW8C3p0B3QhKDMW602vvY53r1N1W4yff421LkQp_VsdhDJ6tMk-6W61mS2j7jlnpbW53JXWT8j_0zQW6_kv3z482_fnW76fTw32V2gGsW1h5ZGh7yf47fW2bTSf-6YCGryW1ZnztF8Lvc3XVMkf-C2c-LgcW1rLj6b1cycqqW8PhzD74nNh97W3GKDKH55KklCN3n-rqyp26K5W1bLhP28C_3r5W4xgzqF22f12MVb2sl06ZDvJmVdr8ht76p5pYf7h8q0s04 )

Anthropic, Cursor & More (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VWCpmr7nxtPTW3FryFd7Ys9CZW5b9Bvk5PxW_NMQqrVs3lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3p1W7DT9CD7zX9CyW17zQLh99k_jkW8C3p0B3QhKDMW602vvY53r1N1W4yff421LkQp_VsdhDJ6tMk-6W61mS2j7jlnpbW53JXWT8j_0zQW6_kv3z482_fnW76fTw32V2gGsW1h5ZGh7yf47fW2bTSf-6YCGryW1ZnztF8Lvc3XVMkf-C2c-LgcW1rLj6b1cycqqW8PhzD74nNh97W3GKDKH55KklCN3n-rqyp26K5W1bLhP28C_3r5W4xgzqF22f12MVb2sl06ZDvJmVdr8ht76p5pYf7h8q0s04 )

Hi Oleg,

The AI race isn’t slowing down. If anything, the numbers just keep getting bigger.

Welcome back to our Daily Pre-IPO Briefing, your quick recap of major headlines involving companies our investors already have exposure to via StartEngine Private.

These headlines span everything from billion-dollar VC rounds to government funding for utility-scale quantum computing:

- Anthropic could close its latest round of funding as soon as this week, with an estimated valuation above $900B. That figure would make Anthropic the world’s most valuable private AI startup. Sequoia Capital, Dragoneer Investment Group, Altimeter Capital, and Greenoaks Capital Partners are expected to co-lead the round and invest roughly $2B each.¹
- Anysphere (the company behind Cursor) achieved $3B ARR in late April. Cursor now has more than 3,000 customers paying at least $100,000 each for its software on an annualized basis. The news follows a deal with SpaceX, which will either acquire Cursor for $60B later in 2026 or pay $10B for an ongoing compute and collaboration partnership.²
- PsiQuantum signed a Letter of Intent with the U.S. Department of Commerce for $100M in federal incentives under the CHIPS and Science Act. Combined with PsiQuantum’s own co-investment, the capital will help the company work toward acheiving utility-scale quantum computing.³

These reports don't imply specific results for investors with exposure to one or more of these companies on StartEngine Private. Past performance is not indicative of future results. There's no guarantee that revenue, funding, or valuation will continue to grow, or that these companies will be profitable. See risks on the offering page.

Private markets continue rewarding companies that can scale infrastructure, revenue, and adoption at the same time. And right now, AI and advanced computing appear to be leading that charge.

Want to further diversify your pre-IPO portfolio? Explore open offerings on StartEngine Private, or schedule a call with a Private Equ

73641.subject: 🟪 Summer reading list
73641.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3f889764-4c4c-426b-b0fe-da4071761d08/BD-Banner-Sponsor_Template.png?t=1779483553)
Follow image link: (https://ore.supply/)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/5eecf506-6251-45b5-ad3a-84ed262e8494/Quote_template_-_Breakdown_NL.png?t=1779831105)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3c08e2dd-b374-47f9-89c3-14b4508e8cb8/image.png?t=1779830962)
Caption: 

# **Summer reading list: Finance edition**

I read six books during my working sabbatical last week. (OK, fine. Two of them I listened to.) Four were recommendations from Matt Levine — his top picks for books about finance, as shared on a recent podcast. One is from Ben Thompson — the best-ever book on Apple and one of the best-ever on China, he says. One is from me — a highly readable history of my favorite thing (money).

I found all of them page-turning enough (each in their own way) to qualify as summer reads. If you similarly think non-fiction finance is suitable for the beach, below are my short takes on each.

## [The Bond King: How One Man Made a Market, Built an Empire, and Lost It All](https://www.amazon.com/Bond-King-Market-Built-Empire-ebook/dp/B08QGKKYX1/ref=sr_1_1?crid=130S0ND1I6BMP&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.Z-96y72sJedk1n9qHJEcZo2BTnj4aAbcSnrZ_xCQJqSIGZ71whf0MlqsHytxZ01i4k5rfuy2REfDkBuTBObchhlouGSJVPSJ2f97D034fQkvvGDkhDkf9VjKaawe2o3Te7RDE1jSRLE80k478XChMJ57phPTHxcCIcXLJDx4OZresEwIPRpIoqqkBs5Ceha_lj1zn-RIHaoe2kj9MuQRoqvrRusUHIfntBA5hfiSPi4.Uns2Fhd2CqxAX_e87NO2We5RqFa8lYf0DzuzMF_By8g&dib_tag=se&keywords=the+bond+king&qid=1779798515&sprefix=the+bond+kin%2Caps%2C253&sr=8-1)

Journalist Mary Childs writes the story of Bill Gross, who invented active management for bond funds.

He was very good at it. Gross was among the first to invest in mortgage-backed securities. He also presciently predicted the demise of mortgage-backed securities well in advance of the 2008 crisis. He once made his investors a killing by cornering the market on a cheapest-to-deliver bond future. He had his traders scour the market for discounted odd lots of bonds to juice returns of an actively managed ETF.

He was not, however, an investing savant.

As depicted by Childs, Gross’s investment process seems pedestrian. He’d sell volatility because he thought the market wouldn’t move much, for example. He’d sell out of Treasuries because he thought the 2009 stimulus would be inflationary. Pretty standard stuff. But he traded in extraordinary size, putting hundreds of billions of dollars behind ideas he got just from thinking about things, basically.

It worked for about two decades. But shortly after his crowning triumph of being short mortgages going into the Great Financial Cris

73637.subject: 🔷 The Daily: UK sanctions HTX
73637.body:
Robinhood clears final regulatory approval for WonderFi deal | Strive leapfrogs Coinbase, Riot with latest $85.4 million bitcoin buy | Hyperliquid rolls out validator-governed prediction markets for real-world events | Nathan Allman, founder and CEO of Ondo Finance, dies unexpectedly |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Daniel Kuhn

Presented by

LMAX ( https://www.theblock.co/lmax?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Tuesday! *Bitcoin's* latest wobble below $77,000 reflects a market growing increasingly sensitive to Iran-related geopolitical headlines, according to analysts ( https://www.theblock.co/post/402552/optimism-looks-fragile-bitcoin-wavers-as-iran-strikes-revive-geopolitical-tensions-and-analysts-warn-of-range-trap?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) , with rising leverage and underwater buyers risking a prolonged $72,000-$82,000 range unless fresh institutional demand returns.

In today's newsletter, the *UK* sanctions *HTX* over support for *Russia* , *Robinhood* clears the final regulatory approval for its acquisition of *WonderFi* , *Ondo Finance's* CEO dies unexpectedly, and more.

Meanwhile, *TeraWulf* stock jumps ( https://www.theblock.co/post/402616/terawulf-stock-jumps-12-news-kentucky-data-center-site-acquisition?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) over 12% on news of its Kentucky data center site acquisition.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read — and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*🇬🇧 UK sanctions HTX over support of Russia in broad sweep over crypto exchanges*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/402591/uk-sanctions-htx-over-support-of-russia-in-broad-sweep-over-crypto-exchanges?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

The *UK* sanctioned crypto exchange *HTX* , formerly known as *Huobi* , accusing the platform of supporting the *Russian government* through financial services linked to sanctions evasion.

* British authorities specifically alleged HTX provided financial services or economic resources to Russian-linked firms, including *A7 LLC* and *Garantex Europe OU*.
* The sanctions form part of a broader UK crackdown targeting crypto exchanges, banks, and companies accused of helping Russia evade international sanctions.
* Blockchain analytics firm *Elliptic* said the move marks the first time the UK has used Regulation 17
